page.title: Christmas Eve Yacht Charter in Marbella: A Magical Night on the Water

data.meta_description: Book a Christmas Eve yacht charter in Marbella for a magical night on the water. From €749/2h, includes skipper, drinks, and festive views of Puerto Banús.
data.summary: Spend Christmas Eve on a private yacht in Marbella with sparkling lights, festive drinks, and stunning coastal views. Charters start at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, fuel, and drinks.
key_facts: Charter from €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39) up to €4,719 for 4 hours (Mangusta 80).
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), snacks, insurance, and IVA.
Departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s iconic marina with Christmas lights and festive atmosphere.
Maximum 12 guests on larger yachts (Mangusta 80, Azimut 58, Ferretti 94); smaller boats for intimate groups.
Available for 2-hour, 4-hour, or full-day charters; pricing on request for most boats via WhatsApp.
Enjoy views of Marbella’s Golden Mile, La Concha mountain, and the Costa del Sol coastline lit up for Christmas.

faq.q: Can I charter a yacht on Christmas Eve in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, Christmas Eve yacht charters are available in Marbella. Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús, and we offer flexible timings from 2-hour trips to full-day excursions. Booking in advance is recommended due to high demand during the festive season.

faq.q: What is included in a Christmas Eve yacht charter?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. The price also covers Spanish IVA (21%). You can bring your own festive treats or decorations with prior arrangement.

faq.q: How much does a Christmas Eve yacht charter cost in Marbella?
faq.a: Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39), rising to €2,299 for 8 hours. For larger groups, the Mangusta 80 (including grey and white variants) costs €4,719 for a minimum 4-hour charter, which includes a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Which boats are available for Christmas Eve charter?
faq.a: We have 18 boats in our fleet, including the Astondoa 40 (9 pax), Azimut 39 (11 pax), Mangusta 80 (12 pax), Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax), Ferretti 94 (12 pax), and more intimate options like the Speedboat (6 pax) or Dubhe (5 pax). All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: What can I see on a Christmas Eve yacht trip in Marbella?
faq.a: You’ll cruise along the Costa del Sol, passing landmarks like the Golden Mile, La Concha mountain, and Marbella Marina. The coastline is decorated with Christmas lights, and Puerto Banús itself is beautifully illuminated. You can also head towards Cabopino or Estepona for quieter views.

faq.q: Is food provided on the Christmas Eve charter?
faq.a: Light snacks are included, such as crisps, nuts, and olives. For a festive touch, you can arrange a catering package or bring your own food. Many guests enjoy tapas or a Christmas Eve dinner onboard—just let us know in advance.

faq.q: Can I bring my own decorations or music for the yacht?
faq.a: Yes, you’re welcome to bring decorations like small lights or a Christmas tree, as long as they don’t damage the boat. Most yachts have Bluetooth speakers or AUX inputs for your own playlist. Check with us beforehand for any restrictions.

faq.q: How do I book a Christmas Eve yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp for pricing and availability for most boats. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) and Mangusta 80, you can book directly on our website. Early booking is advised, especially for larger yachts.

data.body_html:
Christmas Eve in Marbella is a spectacle of lights, sea, and celebration. Instead of a crowded restaurant or a stuffy hotel lounge, imagine gliding across the Mediterranean on a private yacht, champagne in hand, as the Costa del Sol sparkles under a winter sky. A Christmas Eve yacht charter in Marbella offers exactly that—a magical, intimate escape from the festive frenzy, with the added luxury of your own skipper and a fully stocked bar. Whether you’re a couple seeking romance or a group of friends ready to toast under the stars, our fleet departs from Puerto Banús and promises an unforgettable evening.

Why Choose a Christmas Eve Yacht Charter in Marbella?

Christmas Eve in Marbella is mild—expect temperatures around 12–15°C—so being on the water is comfortable with a light jacket. The marina at Puerto Banús is decked out in thousands of twinkling lights, and the yachts themselves add to the glamour. A private charter means you control the itinerary: cruise past the Golden Mile, stop for a swim (yes, the water is still 16°C in December), or simply anchor off Cala del Faro and watch the sunset at 18:00. No queues, no set menus, no rush. It’s just you, your guests, and the sea.

Our Fleet for Christmas Eve

We have 18 boats available, all departing from Puerto Banús. Here are the top picks for a festive evening:

BoatLengthMax PaxPrice (from)
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749/2h
Azimut 3912.5m11€749/2h
Mangusta 8024m12€4,719/4h
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6m12On request
Ferretti 9429m12On request
Speedboat7m6On request

All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA. For the Mangusta 80 variants (grey and white), a jet ski is included free for the 4-hour minimum. For other boats, simply contact us via WhatsApp for a custom quote.

What’s Included in Your Christmas Eve Charter

Every booking comes with a licensed skipper who knows the best spots for sunset views and calm waters. Fuel is covered, so you can cruise without worry. The bar is stocked with water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava—perfect for a Christmas toast. Light snacks (crisps, nuts, olives) are provided, but you’re welcome to bring your own festive buffet. Insurance and Spanish IVA (21%) are included, so no hidden costs. Just show up at Puerto Banús and step aboard.

Best Routes for a Christmas Eve Cruise

From Puerto Banús, you have several options:

  • Marbella Marina & Golden Mile: Head east along the coast to see the Christmas lights of Marbella’s promenade and the grand hotels. The view of La Concha mountain in twilight is spectacular.
  • Cala del Faro & Cala Cortés: West towards Estepona, these secluded coves offer calm waters and a chance to see the lighthouse. Ideal for a quiet anchorage with drinks.
  • Cabopino: A short trip west to the dunes of Cabopino, less crowded than Puerto Banús, with a rustic charm. Great for a sunset toast.
  • Sotogrande: For a longer cruise, head to the luxury marina of Sotogrande, about 20 NM west. Perfect for a full-day charter.

Your skipper will tailor the route to your preferences—whether you want to party or relax.

Tips for a Perfect Christmas Eve Yacht Experience

Book early—Christmas Eve is popular, and larger yachts like the Ferretti 94 or Mangusta 80 get snapped up weeks in advance. Dress in layers: a warm jumper and a windproof jacket are ideal. Bring a camera for the sunset (around 18:00) and the marina lights. If you want a festive touch, pack a small Christmas tree or fairy lights (battery-operated). Many guests also bring a playlist of Christmas classics—our boats have Bluetooth speakers. And don’t forget to arrange a taxi back from Puerto Banús if you plan to enjoy the cava.

How to Book Your Christmas Eve Charter

For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39), you can book directly on our website at the prices listed. For the Mangusta 80 (including grey and white), the minimum 4-hour charter is €4,719 and includes a jet ski. For all other boats, send us a WhatsApp message with your preferred date, group size, and desired duration, and we’ll provide a custom quote within hours. We recommend booking by mid-December to secure your slot.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I charter a yacht on Christmas Eve in Marbella?

Yes, Christmas Eve yacht charters are available in Marbella. Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús, and we offer flexible timings from 2-hour trips to full-day excursions. Booking in advance is recommended due to high demand during the festive season.

What is included in a Christmas Eve yacht charter?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. The price also covers Spanish IVA (21%). You can bring your own festive treats or decorations with prior arrangement.

How much does a Christmas Eve yacht charter cost in Marbella?

Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39), rising to €2,299 for 8 hours. For larger groups, the Mangusta 80 (including grey and white variants) costs €4,719 for a minimum 4-hour charter, which includes a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

Which boats are available for Christmas Eve charter?

We have 18 boats in our fleet, including the Astondoa 40 (9 pax), Azimut 39 (11 pax), Mangusta 80 (12 pax), Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax), Ferretti 94 (12 pax), and more intimate options like the Speedboat (6 pax) or Dubhe (5 pax). All depart from Puerto Banús.

What can I see on a Christmas Eve yacht trip in Marbella?

You’ll cruise along the Costa del Sol, passing landmarks like the Golden Mile, La Concha mountain, and Marbella Marina. The coastline is decorated with Christmas lights, and Puerto Banús itself is beautifully illuminated. You can also head towards Cabopino or Estepona for quieter views.

Is food provided on the Christmas Eve charter?

Light snacks are included, such as crisps, nuts, and olives. For a festive touch, you can arrange a catering package or bring your own food. Many guests enjoy tapas or a Christmas Eve dinner onboard—just let us know in advance.

Can I bring my own decorations or music for the yacht?

Yes, you’re welcome to bring decorations like small lights or a Christmas tree, as long as they don’t damage the boat. Most yachts have Bluetooth speakers or AUX inputs for your own playlist. Check with us beforehand for any restrictions.

How do I book a Christmas Eve yacht charter in Marbella?

Contact us via WhatsApp for pricing and availability for most boats. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) and Mangusta 80, you can book directly on our website. Early booking is advised, especially for larger yachts.
